seed_problem: Select a maximum-weight set of mutually non-conflicting research sites on a general conflict graph; checker validates independence and scores weight relative to a baseline.
mutation: input: relax the conflict graph from a special (e.g. bipartite/interval) structure to a general graph built from conflict-cliques plus random cross edges, so maximum-weight independent set is NP-hard and weight-greedy / weight-per-degree / local-search heuristics diverge.
title: Grain-Lot Slate: Conflict-Free Sourcing
strategies: index-order reference: scan lots 1..n, keep each that conflicts with nothing kept so far (the scored baseline)
weight-greedy: consider lots in descending profit, add if it conflicts with no chosen lot
weight-per-degree greedy: order lots by profit divided by (conflict degree + 1) to favour high-value low-conflict lots
local-search swaps: seed with weight-greedy, then repeatedly add a lot while evicting the lighter conflicting chosen lots whenever that raises total profit
randomized multi-restart: perturb the greedy ordering across restarts and keep the highest-profit conflict-free slate
